## Enterprise Railway Maintenance Scheduling Software Solutions (2025)

### Major Enterprise Solutions

**Trapeze EAM**
As an enterprise asset management (EAM) solution, Trapeze bills itself as the best solution for maintaining proper repair schedules and managing funds.(no content)Trapeze is one of the few EAMs designed specifically for public transportation, so if you're required to meet stringent public safety standards, this solution is best suited for your organization.

**Hitachi Energy**
Hitachi Energy's software solutions can help shift your maintenance planning from reactive to preventative.(no content)Railway software can be used to monitor the condition of railway infrastructure, identify areas that need maintenance or repairs, and schedule maintenance work. This helps to reduce the likelihood of breakdowns and accidents, which can lead to delays and additional energy consumption.

**Rail Transportation Management Systems (RTMS)**
A Rail Transportation Management System is a digital platform that manages rail operations across all facets of scheduling, asset tracking, freight movement, and maintenance across the entire rail network.(no content)IoT sensors with analytics are enabling predictive maintenance in railways by recognizing potential equipment failures before they worsen. Meanwhile, railroad equipment catalogue software provides detailed information on parts, inspections, and repairs.

**Kaleris Enterprise Rail Management**
More than 700 connected shops use Kaleris to digitalize every aspect of the maintenance process.(no content)Improve fleet utilization through end-to-end visibility on maintenance records, inspection records, defects, and repair progression.

**Trimble E2M**
Configure fleets including the asset tree, create assets and define maintenance programs to create a complete maintenance schedule.(no content)Centralized asset management systems bring defect reporting, maintenance management and planning together in one place for both asset owners and franchisee operators and maintainers.

**voestalpine zentrak**
zentrak Asset and Maintenance Management helps you to identify the ideal maintenance strategy mix for your assets. It enables reactive, preventive and prescriptive maintenance activities.(no content)Our advanced SaaS solution transforms unstructured asset and maintenance data into valuable decision-making insights.

**Railnova**
Railfleet enables preventive and corrective maintenance scheduling based on diagnostics, telematic counters, time limits and tolerance.(no content)Thanks to Railnova, railway fleet managers have the best tools at their disposal to digitalise their maintenance processes. Send defect reports and dispatch online maintenance work orders to your own or external maintenance teams.

**RAILSoft by Petrosoft.pl**
RAILSoft is an Enterprise Resource Planning system designed to enhance the effectiveness of railway companies.(no content)Focusing on the maintenance and railway safety functionalities, RAILSoft manages the technical condition of wagons, locomotives, and railway infrastructure. It allows for the planning and management of mandatory technical inspections and collects inspection documentation.

### Key Industry Players
Key users, including railway operators, maintenance teams and traffic controllers, rely on these systems for schedule management, maintenance execution and ensuring safe train operations. Major vendors in this space include ABB, Hitachi Rail, CloudMoyo, and Hexagon, among others.

Based on my search, I found information about both **Railnova** and **Signature Rail**, which are two separate companies providing railway software solutions:

## Railnova

Railnova is a technology company that provides both fleet and maintenance workflow software and telematics solutions to the railway industry. They develop and sell a comprehensive and multi-company fleet management solution for operators, lessors, maintainers and manufacturers.

Their key products include:

- **Railfleet**: Railnova's fleet management platform that allows you to see all your trains in one place, and to communicate about fleet condition, status, availability and maintenance planning in real time. It helps you to report new defects, to manage campaigns and automatically schedule preventive maintenance based on telematic counters, tolerance and time limits.

- **Railgenius**: Railnova's real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance platform. It enables you to apply logic to the data coming from your rolling stock and to turn it into actionable predictive alerts.

- **Railster**: Railnova's remote monitoring and edge device. It enables you to safely connect any type of rolling stock or component and stream data to your IT cloud in real time.(no content)Railnova's customers include blue-chip companies like Eurotunnel, Lineas, and DB Cargo.

## Signature Rail

Signature Rail provides timetable and resource planning software for train operating companies. They are specialists in planning software, used by train operating companies to generate robust conflict-free train timetables and validated resource plans.(no content)Signature Rail solutions support rail operators from strategic planning through to short term planning and the management of disruption on the day of operations. They enable customers to make assured and fully informed decisions that reduce costs, carbon footprint and risk.(no content)Their planning solutions provide tracking of fuel, maintenance distances and crew hours and costs.

Note: These appear to be two separate companies - Railnova focuses on fleet management, monitoring, and predictive maintenance, while Signature Rail specializes in timetable and resource planning software. I did not find any product specifically called "SignatureRail" from Railnova.

Here are the search results for "metro fleet management system train maintenance planner":

## Metro and Rail Fleet Management Systems

There are several fleet management and maintenance planning systems available for metro and rail operations:

### ZEDAS Asset Management
zedas®asset is an integrated solution for the efficient management and maintenance of rail vehicle fleets. The railway-specific CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) supports the economical operation and maintenance of all rail vehicles.(no content)The zedas®asset Resource Manager supports the workshop and service team in planning all maintenance activities and the required resources. Qualifications and certificates for professional maintenance are managed in the system and considered during planning. Free resources and planning conflicts are highlighted, and bottlenecks are identified at an early stage.

### MaintWiz AI CMMS
MaintWiz AI CMMS drives unparalleled efficiency in rolling stock maintenance, track upkeep, and signaling system reliability to keep railways on the fast track to success.(no content)It can orchestrate maintenance activities across your entire metro network with centralized planning and execution tools.

### DELMIA Quintiq Rail Fleet Management
DELMIA Quintiq offers integrated end-to-end multidisciplinary planning approaches that reduce problem complexity by eliminating silo decision-making, and plans network, services, rolling stock plan, and crew schedules.(no content)It creates efficient rolling stock plans by increasing rolling stock utilization, reducing empty repositioning, and delivering a robust rolling stock plan by considering rolling stock balancing, maintenance, and availability.

### Railnova (Railfleet)
Railfleet is Railnova's digital ECM fleet and maintenance management platform. It enables you to view all your trains in one place and to communicate about fleet condition, availability and maintenance planning with your stakeholders. Railfleet also enables preventive and corrective maintenance scheduling based on diagnostics, telematic counters, time limits and tolerance.

### Binary System MainTrack
A Digital Maintenance Management solution enables rail operators to efficiently monitor and plan all aspects of preventive and corrective maintenance.(no content)Binary System offers a Digital Maintenance Management solution called MainTrack, which is part of its SoftRail suite. Developed in collaboration with maintenance experts, MainTrack enables Fleet Management Teams, Drivers and Cabin Crew, and Workshop Staff to streamline every aspect of their maintenance operation.

### IVU Fleet Management
IVU.fleet communicates continuously with the driver-operated IVU.cockpit on-board computer software in buses and trains. Route changes, auxiliary vehicle schedules or aborted journeys can be created with just a few clicks and automatically forwarded to the bus or train.

### Transit Agency Fleet Management Plans
Metrolink's Rail Fleet Management Plan (RFMP) is a living document which contains the strategy for achieving and maintaining vehicle assets in a state of good repair, charting a course for future service and investment decisions related to vehicle fleet and facilities through FY2040.(no content)WMATA Metro is improving its railcar rehabilitation program by implementing the SMP to overhaul railcar systems and components on a six-year cycle in lieu of the midlife overhaul program. Metro also modified the timing and tasks associated with the PMI (Preventive Maintenance & Inspection) program. These changes, coupled with the replacement of older and less reliable railcars, have improved fleet reliability.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about the Railnova Railfleet dashboard interface:

## Railnova Railfleet Dashboard Overview

Railfleet is Railnova's digital ECM fleet and maintenance management platform. It enables you to view all your trains in one place and to communicate about fleet condition, availability and maintenance planning with your stakeholders.

### Key Dashboards in Railfleet

**Operational Dashboard:**
The Operational Dashboard is the dashboard that displays all your operational fleet information in one place. It gives your fleet managers, ECMs and other stakeholders real-time access to important operational data such as asset status, restrictions, rolling stock location, key telematic values, events and fleet availability notifications.(no content)To access the dashboard, go to the Railnova platform, then head over to the "Railfleet" section and click the "Operational" link.

**Asset Status Indicators:**
A simple action you can take on the dashboard is viewing and updating the operational status of assets. You can verify if an asset is operational (green), in degraded mode (yellow), immobilised (red) or not yet defined (black).

**Planning Dashboard:**
The planning dashboard gives you as an operator or ECM a complete overview of all maintenance events that have been created for your rolling stock. You efficiently follow up on the state of maintenance events (unplanned, planned, overdue, closed) per asset and bundle corrective and preventive events into a maintenance intervention.

### Demo Access

Want to see how Railfleet works? Create an account, or contact us for a live demo!(no content)You can discover Railfleet's new look right away on their platform at https://me.railnova.eu.

The search results describe the dashboard features but don't include actual screenshots. You would need to visit the Railnova platform directly or request a demo from them to see the actual interface visuals.

Based on my search results, here's an overview of rolling stock maintenance management KPI metrics visualization:

## Maintenance KPIs for Rolling Stock and Asset Management

### Key Metrics to Track

The primary maintenance performance indicators include MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures), MTTR (Mean Time to Repair), system availability, and maintenance costs, which provide essential insights into the reliability and operational readiness of assets.(no content)Schedule Compliance is a common maintenance KPI metric showing the percentage of all scheduled maintenance tasks your maintenance team finished by the scheduled deadline.

### Visualization Tools and Dashboards

Dashboards offer visual tools that aggregate KPI data for quick reference and analysis to provide a clear, real-time view of key metrics. The layout helps teams stay informed and responsive to whatever is needed at any given moment.(no content)CMMS software provides advanced reporting and analytics capabilities. Through visualizations such as graphs and charts, these quantifiable benchmarks help organizations track a specific KPI for a maintenance department.(no content)Modern systems feature customizable KPI displays that highlight critical metrics, automated data collection that reduces manual input errors, visual indicators that quickly flag metrics falling outside acceptable ranges, and historical trending data to identify long-term performance patterns. By consolidating these metrics into accessible dashboards, maintenance teams can spot potential issues before they escalate into costly breakdowns or production delays.

### Railway-Specific Standards

Well known standards for maintenance KPIs include the European Standards EN 15341 and SMRP Best Practice metrics.(no content)Performance indicators of railway infrastructure have been categorized into two groups: managerial and condition monitoring indicators.

### Best Practices for Implementation

At any one time, you will want to have no more than five KPIs. Remember, these are your top priorities. If you create too many, they will simply become metrics and you will dilute your resources.(no content)With real-time visibility into metrics such as Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) or Maintenance Backlog, teams can move away from reactive firefighting and toward proactive decision-making. CMMS platforms integrate analytics, making it easier to visualize performance trends and identify gaps before they become critical problems.(no content)Advanced maintenance management software solutions offer features for data aggregation, visualization, trend analysis, and predictive analytics, enabling organizations to gain deeper insights into maintenance performance, identify opportunities for improvement, and make informed decisions to optimize operations.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about ZEDAS asset management for rail vehicle maintenance interfaces:

## ZEDAS Asset Management Overview

zedas®asset is the standard solution for asset and maintenance management of rail vehicles and rail infrastructure. The EAM (Enterprise Asset Management) integrates all stakeholders into one system and digitally maps all typical maintenance processes.

### Key Interface Features

Data from third-party systems are imported into zedas®asset via interfaces and are then available for central data processing. Electronic data exchange with peripheral systems decisively supports business processes by reducing the effort required to obtain information. zedas®asset already offers numerous standard interfaces that significantly reduce the implementation effort.(no content)Individual interfaces to third-party systems are provided on request.

### Standard Interface Types

The system supports interfaces to ERP systems for material (material basic data, reservation, BANF), basic data (accounts, creditor, debtor, personal), staff timekeeping, orders, fuel data systems, and measured data systems (integration of measured data from different systems – devices, trolleys, vehicles, etc.).(no content)Additional interfaces include counter import (integration of different measured data and counters such as loads, vehicles per day, etc.) and fault/error import (integration of error codes or fault messages from other systems).

### Mobile Interfaces

Documentation of material costs, working hours, resources used, recording counter readings and faults, filling in checklists, and releasing vehicles can all be done with zedas®asset Touch directly on the train on a tablet or desktop PC. An easy-to-understand user interface supports workshop personnel in fulfilling their tasks and documentation obligations.(no content)The zedas®asset Smart app makes it possible to digitally record faults directly on the vehicle. The app offers an intuitive fault catalogue and supports mobile service teams, with GPS positioning and the ability to store photos of damage for fast and precise maintenance.

### Integration with Logistics

ZEDAS provides the leading product suite for the seamless connection of logistics management of rail freight transport (zedas®cargo) with fleet and maintenance management (zedas®asset). The automated information flow significantly reduces the need for coordination between areas. Data on individual rail vehicles obtained during the operating process is available for maintenance planning.

### Advanced Technologies

zedas®asset uses innovative technologies such as IoT, AI and machine learning to predict wheelset wear, weak points, personnel requirements and investment needs.(no content)ZEDAS GmbH is working with BLG RailTec GmbH and the Brandenburg University of Technology on an augmented reality application (AR) for data glasses and tablets specifically for railway workshops. With the AR application, it is possible to relate a wagon in the workshop and an order from the asset management system in three dimensions.

Based on my search, here's an overview of CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) software features for railway maintenance, including dashboard and preventive maintenance capabilities:

## Key Features of CMMS for Railway Maintenance

### Preventive Maintenance
CMMS plays a crucial role in optimizing maintenance practices, resulting in significant cost savings for railway companies. By facilitating preventive maintenance, it helps minimize expensive repairs and unexpected downtime, thereby reducing the likelihood of equipment failures.(no content)It provides a centralized preventive maintenance platform for creating work orders, assigning tasks, and tracking progress. This ensures timely and proactive maintenance, reducing the risk of equipment failures and minimizing downtime.(no content)Railway CMMS allows you to plan corrective, preventive and predictive maintenance activities. Complete your maintenance orders with the help of checklists and work packages. Document condition information on the vehicle, maintenance activities and component changes as well as the consumption of resources automatically.

### Dashboard Capabilities
CMMS solutions offer real-time dashboards, enabling instant data access, faster response times, proactive maintenance, and cost savings.(no content)CMMS dashboards show a snapshot of real-time data, helping maintenance managers make informed decisions.

### Asset Management & Tracking
CMMS helps in effectively managing railway assets. It enables maintenance technicians to record and track asset information, including maintenance history, warranties, and specifications. This facilitates better decision-making regarding repairs, replacements, and asset lifecycle management.

### Mobile Access

## Train Maintenance Planning & Scheduling

### Railway-Specific Maintenance Approaches

Condition-based monitoring is the most modern method used to determine train maintenance schedules, relying on automated data collection and sensors to trigger maintenance checks and processes based on the real-world performance of the vehicle.(no content)Rolling stock maintenance traditionally followed time-based methods, with functions such as braking safety and wheel condition evaluated on a pre-determined schedule. Later, mileage-based methods became commonplace to perform maintenance based on actual vehicle usage rather than specified time periods.(no content)Maintenance schedules must also be coordinated with utilization schedules to ensure efficient operations, making the use of comprehensive planning and scheduling software desirable for modern railway operators.

### Work Order Management in Rail Operations

Whatever monitoring and maintenance scheduling system is used, there comes a point when service orders need to be created. Parts not held in stock need ordering, and parts taken from stock need replenishing. Many rail operators use SAP Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) to run their business functions, including generating service and purchase orders.(no content)XV is described as "the UK's leading railway maintenance management product enabling depots to maintain what they need to, when they need to, rather than adhering to a set maintenance schedule." XV reduces costs by increasing the efficiency of data input and management, allowing defects to be tracked, categorized, and analyzed.

### Calendar View & Scheduling Software Features

Modern planning software allows you to track upcoming activities, have all necessary resources at hand, and efficiently assign tasks with a dynamic work order calendar.(no content)These systems let you view every scheduled activity on a single screen with accurate workload insights for each team member, and use a Gantt view to compare schedules and assign tasks strategically.(no content)The Visual Planner Digital Platform is a maintenance planning and scheduling solution comprised of graphical applications that offer a unique calendar approach. It is specifically designed to manage all steps of the scheduling process, from long-term to short-term, with a rigorous multi-week rolling schedule and daily work execution.

### Integrated Train Schedule & Maintenance Planning

Train timetabling, platforming, and network maintenance scheduling are three highly interdependent problems that are crucial in the planning of railway operations, and each is normally addressed separately.(no content)To limit the impact of maintenance operations on network capacity, the time windows for maintenance are typically placed during the night. Given these time windows, the train operation department can design a yearly, monthly, or weekly train schedule.(no content)Railway track maintenance and renewal are vital for railway safety, train punctuality, and travel comfort. Therefore, having cost-effective maintenance is critical in managing railway infrastructure assets.

### Calendar-Based Maintenance in CMMS

Modern CMMS systems offer automated work order generation, creating work orders based on predefined calendar schedules without manual intervention. They enable recurring maintenance setup with tasks that repeat at specified calendar intervals (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or yearly).(no content)CMMS provides graphical calendar views of all scheduled maintenance activities for better planning and resource allocation, with flexible scheduling options allowing maintenance scheduling by specific dates, days of the week, weeks of the month, or other calendar patterns.

## Train Fleet Predictive Maintenance with IoT

### Rail-Specific Solutions

Real-time, single-source customizable dashboards and/or alerting systems are supported by TransmetriQ technology that processes data from thousands of wayside detectors and onboard GPS sensors.(no content)This technology analyzes the flow of rail-car data combined with information about current network conditions and historical performance data.(no content)Progress Rail offers the Uptime™ suite, a comprehensive predictive analytics platform for condition-based locomotive maintenance.(no content)Railnova's Railster is a remote monitoring and edge device that enables you to safely connect any type of rolling stock or component and stream data to your IT cloud in real time.(no content)The updatable software on the Railster allows you to perform edge computing and compute your own rules, in order to receive custom fault codes and alerts.(no content)Railgenius is Railnova's monitoring and predictive diagnostics platform.

### How IoT Predictive Maintenance Works

Connected IoT sensors can provide real-time data on vehicle parts and send Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) to track mechanical failures in real time while fleet maintenance management systems use this data for analytics and predictive maintenance.(no content)Temperature shifts, vibration changes, or pressure drops can alert maintenance teams to problems weeks ahead of breakdowns.(no content)The US Department of Energy reports that predictive maintenance programs generate returns 10 times higher than their initial investment.

### Dashboard and Alert Systems

User-friendly dashboards provide fleet managers with actionable insights, alerts, and recommendations. These tools simplify decision-making by visualizing complex data in an intuitive format.(no content)Systems must develop algorithms that distinguish between normal equipment variations and potential problems, accounting for different usage patterns, seasonal operating conditions, and individual equipment characteristics to provide accurate maintenance alerts without overwhelming teams with false positives.(no content)Predictive vehicle fleet maintenance software can provide alerts on potential issues several days to months in advance. A smart alert system integrated within fleet maintenance software can accelerate fleet managers' and drivers' reactions in the field, sending alerts to make fast decisions on taking a vehicle off the road, visiting a nearby repair shop, or finishing the route.

### Key Technologies

A robust predictive maintenance system relies on IoT sensors, telematics, AI algorithms, cloud computing platforms, and user-friendly analytics dashboards.(no content)Teams need tools that translate AI insights into visual dashboards, alerts, and reports that are easy to understand and act on.

## Trapeze EAM - Transit & Rail Asset Management Software

### Overview
Trapeze EAM is the only system built specifically for public transportation that helps transit agencies manage their assets. Trapeze EAM helps you manage your fleet, facilities, and wayside assets to meet regulations and stay ahead of industry trends.

### Key Interface Features

**Real-Time Monitoring & Display**
- By displaying real-time vehicle status and current location to Maintenance and Operations, EAM streamlines on-time departure.
- Improve response times to operational asset faults and failures through real-time asset status views using remote condition monitoring technology.

**Integration Capabilities**
- Interface seamlessly with building management systems to improve asset-failure response times.
- The Trapeze EAM solution can also integrate with intelligent, self-monitoring assets to enable predictive maintenance.
- The joint initiative integrates Sentient Science's rail maintenance software within Trapeze's EAM, capital planning and state of good repair tools.

**Rail-Specific Features**
- Record starting and stopping location of track work in real time
- Illustrated Parts Catalog for graphical parts selection
- Run compliance checks to ensure signal firmware is up to date and in line with Positive Train Control (PTC) requirements
- The Trapeze Enterprise Asset Management solution incorporates workflows and processes unique to rail, tracking assets and infrastructure including rolling stock, facilities, linear assets, signalling and other components.

**Mobile Access**
- Maximize access to asset condition information with EAM Mobile – online or offline

**Condition-Based & Predictive Maintenance**
- A key feature of a powerful, tailor-made asset management solution is that it helps rail operators and maintainers move away from planned maintenance activities toward condition-based and predictive maintenance strategies. Trapeze EAM does this by tracking an asset's condition throughout its useful life. Information is power and understanding your asset condition – in real time – enables maintenance plans and inspections to be adjusted specifically to ensure assets keep operating at acceptable levels by intervening only when required.

**Incident Management**
- See the current performance of rail operations and maintenance through incident management capabilities.

**Work Order Management**
- EAM radically improves the efficiencies of supervisors, technicians, and storekeepers by streamlining work order coordination and accurate data collection.

**Compliance & Reporting**
- EAM's out of the box reports streamline compliance with the Federal Transit Administration regulations.

### Industry Adoption
Join 100+ peer agencies who rely on EAM to improve the performance and reliability of their fleet, facilities, and infrastructure assets. Case studies include MBTA reduced workflow completion time by 50% after optimizing the Network Restrictions module.

Here are the search results for railway asset condition status color coding visual indicators maintenance:

## General Asset Maintenance Color Coding

Visual status indicators enable faster decision-making and help maintenance teams focus on the highest-priority assets first.(no content)With tools like Atlas, you can color-code assets by maintenance status in minutes, and everything happens visually, directly on your map.(no content)Color-coding assets by maintenance status is useful for facility managers tracking building systems and equipment maintenance, fleet managers monitoring vehicle service schedules and compliance, manufacturing maintaining production equipment and preventing downtime, and utilities managing infrastructure assets across service territories.

## Railway-Specific Condition Monitoring Systems

Several companies offer condition monitoring solutions for railway assets:

**ZF connect@rail:**
This digital condition monitoring system enables predictive maintenance planning for rail vehicles and infrastructure. Sensors are mounted on the bogie of rail vehicles to record accelerations, vibrations and anomalies in wheel-rail contact during operation, and the data is then analyzed and visualized in the cloud.

**Progress Rail:**
Argus collects and compiles data from various types of wayside vehicle performance monitoring devices to create a composite view of railway vehicle performance. This view can be selectively modified by the user to create custom event notifications and reports for individual vehicle performance or overall fleet performance.(no content)OSCAR provides an automated maintenance reporting tool to predict problems with rolling stock and any other critical asset components. OSCAR acts as a repository for Asset Protection data for Asset Protection, Asset Utilisation and Asset Maintenance.

**Wabtec:**
Condition monitoring allows users to use condition data to schedule and perform maintenance effectively and to maximize asset life. It helps detect faults and identify early indicators that may lead to safety incidents to help mitigate incidents and improve safety, while gaining real-time insights into assets and operations.

**AP Sensing SmartVision:**
DAS and DTS integrate seamlessly into SmartVision, an asset visualization and data management platform. SmartVision consolidates real-time data from multiple sensors, providing operators with a clear overview of the railway network, including asset condition and environmental status. This integration helps optimize railway operations, improve safety, and support predictive maintenance strategies.

**Trimble:**
Trimble Nexala software solutions utilize on-train equipment and cloud-based software to deliver real-time fleet-wide remote diagnostic information. The software processes vast quantities of raw data in real-time, providing a comprehensive view of overall fleet status and location.

## Deep Learning and Automated Monitoring

Rail inspection vehicles and sensor technologies are being deployed as efficient and cost-effective data collection technology solutions to support rail maintenance operations and can capture vast amounts of data. Data-driven automatic condition monitoring and detection and classification of rail track anomalies have been attracting attention from researchers at universities and railway institutes.

The search results show that while condition monitoring and visualization are well-established in railway maintenance, specific color-coding schemes for asset condition status (such as red/yellow/green indicators) are not explicitly detailed in these sources. The focus is more on digital dashboards, cloud-based visualization, and data-driven predictive maintenance approaches rather than standardized color coding schemes.

Here are the search results for SAP railway transportation maintenance work orders ERP interface:

## SAP Solutions for Railway Transportation and Maintenance

### SAP Transportation Management (TM) for Rail

With SAP TM, it is possible to map a digitalized order and freight cost management and thus reduce transport costs while optimizing material and goods flows. For the railway application area this means mapping process-relevant master data for resources, schedules, business rules and freight costs and using it for transport planning, processing and billing. The advantage here is the high degree of integration with SAP ERP or S/4HANA.(no content)TM offers the basic function of transferring transport requirements from SAP ERP to TM and mapping them in the form of divisible freight units. The wagon unit is a further development to a pure freight unit: If the transport decision is made for the railroad, a wagon unit can be created directly instead of a freight unit.

### Railway Maintenance Integration

At Etihad Rail, the SAP PM (Plant Maintenance) module — now known as SAP EAM (Enterprise Asset Management) — was implemented as the central system for digital asset management. The mobile maintenance solution is based on SAP Fiori and was fully integrated into the existing SAP system via SAP Gateway — without third-party solutions or media discontinuities.(no content)The maintenance teams use SAP Fiori-based interfaces on tablets for digital reporting — directly at the point of operation. Etihad Rail and valantic implemented a fully digital maintenance solution using SAP PM and Fiori. The mobile platform supports asset management along 264 km of railway through the UAE desert and is ready for future network expansion.

### Data-Driven Maintenance and Work Orders

Data-driven maintenance for rolling stock integrates sensor analytics with SAP Asset Management. This end-to-end approach covers real-time monitoring to automated work orders.(no content)SAP's Strategic Direction 2025 for railways focuses on an AI-First approach, using AI to optimize maintenance, scheduling, and operations. With a Suite-First strategy, SAP aims to provide integrated solutions that enhance efficiency, reliability, and cost-effectiveness across the rail network.

### ERP Interface Considerations

Via the logistics modules SAP ERP, S/4HANA or another upstream system, you can transfer sales orders, purchase orders and deliveries to SAP TM via XML.(no content)Ensuring that SAP continues to integrate and interface with key external packages is a critical part of any transition project; from revenue recognition/ticketing systems through to clock-in/clock out systems which drive payroll and fleet maintenance packages.(no content)The particular advantage of integrated systems and standard software, such as SAP TM, is that they are part of the ERP environment and the complete SAP suite. As a result, all information required for planning, controlling, monitoring and optimizing transport processes within a supply network is available transparently and standardized in one application. This also includes data from ordering, production, accounting, purchasing, warehousing and inventory management.

### Recent Developments

ProRail, the Dutch railway infrastructure manager, initiated the Barista project to transition to SAP S/4HANA, aiming to enhance agility and streamline operations. They successfully implemented the new platform on Azure Cloud within a four-month timeline, improving processes in finance, purchasing, project management, and reporting.

Based on my search, here's what I found about rolling stock maintenance work order forms and digital checklist inspection solutions:

## Maintenance Work Order Forms & Digital Checklists

### What is a Maintenance Work Order Form?

A maintenance work order is a paper or digital form that outlines the maintenance work to be performed. It provides essential details about the job, such as the scope of work, materials needed, assigned personnel, and deadlines. Work orders help ensure that maintenance activities are properly tracked, prioritized, and completed.

### Types of Work Orders

Inspection Work Orders are used for routine inspections to ensure equipment is in good condition. Routine Maintenance Work Orders cover simple upkeep tasks such as cleaning and lubrication.

### Key Elements of a Work Order Form

A well-designed maintenance work order form should include:
- Work order number (a unique identifier for tracking), work order description (a clear explanation of the task), priority level (indicates urgency), and work order status to track whether the WO is scheduled, in progress, or completed.
- Photos or attachments can be added to digital forms, including supporting images or documents like OEM manuals, troubleshooting diagrams, or SOPs. Safety procedures and approval signatures should also be included.

### Benefits of Digital/CMMS Solutions

No matter your industry—from manufacturing to facility management to automotive—using a digital solution helps ensure technicians don't commit mechanical errors. You can issue work orders with the tap of a button using a CMMS. When you issue a work order form, technicians are notified immediately. You can include procedures, graphics, and notes along with the work order. Technicians can mark completed tasks as they move through a procedure, and you'll have complete visibility over the work order's progress.

### Digital Checklist Features

Checklists and inspection management tools are included in modern CMMS platforms like MaintainX. This software makes the process of creating, assigning, completing, and tracking maintenance and inspections more efficient by allowing teams to convert paper forms into standardized, easy-to-access digital templates. This ultimately ensures consistency and provides an audit trail.(no content)Systems can automate failed inspection checklists to trigger follow-up work orders and enable operators and technicians to easily log meter readings for runtime-based preventive maintenance tasks, or include photos to checklists.

### Available Solutions and Templates

Several platforms offer maintenance work order and inspection solutions:
- **FMX** - Offers a free, customizable work order form template that gives teams the foundation needed to successfully prioritize and execute every maintenance task.
- **MaintainX** - Features a global procedure library, housing procedures, manuals, and templates across industries and equipment.
- **UpKeep** - Allows creating templates for recurring checklists or to use across multiple work orders, helping standardize project management across multiple facilities.
- **ReachOut** - Helps convert paper-based work order forms to digital replicas with customized inspection forms.

The search results focused on general maintenance work orders and inspection forms. For solutions specifically tailored to **railway rolling stock** maintenance, you may want to search for specialized rail industry CMMS providers or contact rail-specific software vendors.

Based on my search results, I found extensive research on metro train depot resource planning, workshop capacity, and scheduling. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Metro Train Depot Resource Planning, Workshop Capacity & Scheduling

### Overview of the Planning Problem

The planning of railway operations consists of several interconnected planning problems. Due to the complexity, the problems are often seen as independent stages that can be solved individually. The most important planning problems are line planning, train timetabling, train platforming, rolling stock scheduling, and crew scheduling.

### Depot Location and Capacity Optimization

Researchers are addressing the problem of selecting new depot locations and their corresponding capacities. A two-stage mixed integer programming approach for rolling stock scheduling with maintenance requirements has been extended to account for depot selection.(no content)To ensure the safe running of trains, 10 large depots and more than 50 small depots are scattered throughout the Chinese High-Speed Railway (CHSR) network as maintenance bases.

### Shared Depot Concept

The shared depot refers to the train depot serving multiple metro lines simultaneously, allowing train units to operate across different lines at different times, thus leading to more efficient utilization of resources within metro networks.

### Rolling Stock Scheduling with Multiple Depots

Train scheduling and rolling stock circulation planning are two critical procedures in planning the operation schemes for the metro system, in which the time-varying passenger demand is usually incorporated in order to improve the service level.(no content)Each depot is connected with the operating stations along the metro line. A certain number of rolling stock units are pre-stored in different depots, and the total number of in-service rolling stock units should not exceed the number of available vehicles in the system.

### Key Constraints and Considerations

A key problem is that the available rolling stock resources at each depot may not be enough to compose required train platoons for crowded lines. To this end, the departure times of trains should be appropriately designed to meet the requirement of rolling stock circulations. For example, if a train plans to depart from depot d, then there must be sufficient vehicle units in that depot to compose the next train.

### Integrated Optimization Approaches

Urban rail transit train operation plan is a comprehensive production plan encompassing line planning, timetabling, and rolling stock scheduling. To solve the problem of infeasibility of the line plan and timetable because the number of rolling stocks could be only precisely considered in the rolling stock scheduling, an integrated optimization solution is proposed which considers the line plan, timetable, and rolling stock schedule.

### Advanced Methods

Researchers are studying the integration of train scheduling and rolling stock circulation planning under time-varying passenger demand, where practical train operation constraints such as the capacity of trains, the number of available rolling stocks, and the entering/exiting depot operations are considered. Solution approaches for multi-objective mixed-integer nonlinear programming problems deliver both an irregular train schedule and a rolling stock circulation plan simultaneously.(no content)Mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) models are being developed that simultaneously optimize the train-platoon (de)coupling strategies, arrival/departure times at each station, and running orders of trains, while considering limited rolling stock resources at the depots and the safety of trains at cross-line zones.

A two-stage mixed integer programming approach for rolling stock scheduling with maintenance requirements has been extended to account for depot selection.(no content)To ensure the safe running of trains, 10 large depots and more than 50 small depots are scattered throughout the Chinese High-Speed Railway (CHSR) network as maintenance bases.

### Shared Depot Concept

The shared depot refers to the train depot serving multiple metro lines simultaneously, allowing train units to operate across different lines at different times, thus leading to more efficient utilization of resources within metro networks.

### Rolling Stock Scheduling with Multiple Depots

Train scheduling and rolling stock circulation planning are two critical procedures in planning the operation schemes for the metro system, in which the time-varying passenger demand is usually incorporated in order to improve the service level.(no content)Each depot is connected with the operating stations along the metro line. A certain number of rolling stock units are pre-stored in different depots, and the total number of in-service rolling stock units should not exceed the number of available vehicles in the system.

### Key Constraints and Considerations

A key problem is that the available rolling stock resources at each depot may not be enough to compose required train platoons for crowded lines. To this end, the departure times of trains should be appropriately designed to meet the requirement of rolling stock circulations. For example, if a train plans to depart from depot d, then there must be sufficient vehicle units in that depot to compose the next train.

### Integrated Optimization Approaches

Urban rail transit train operation plan is a comprehensive production plan encompassing line planning, timetabling, and rolling stock scheduling. To solve the problem of infeasibility of the line plan and timetable because the number of rolling stocks could be only precisely considered in the rolling stock scheduling, an integrated optimization solution is proposed which considers the line plan, timetable, and rolling stock schedule.

### Advanced Methods

Researchers are studying the integration of train scheduling and rolling stock circulation planning under time-varying passenger demand, where practical train operation constraints such as the capacity of trains, the number of available rolling stocks, and the entering/exiting depot operations are considered. Solution approaches for multi-objective mixed-integer nonlinear programming problems deliver both an irregular train schedule and a rolling stock circulation plan simultaneously.(no content)Mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) models are being developed that simultaneously optimize the train-platoon (de)coupling strategies, arrival/departure times at each station, and running orders of trains, while considering limited rolling stock resources at the depots and the safety of trains at cross-line zones.

Based on my search results, here is information about ECM (Entity in Charge of Maintenance) in the railway industry and related software/digital platforms:

## ECM (Entity in Charge of Maintenance) Overview

The ECM – the entity in charge of maintenance – plays an important role in the European rail transport system.(no content)In the National Vehicle Register (NVR), an ECM is allocated to each vehicle. This entity is responsible for implementing a maintenance system that ensures the safe operating condition of the rail vehicle and thus contributes essentially to railway safety.

### ECM Functions (Categories)

The concept of ECM is divided into four parts or functions, which are referred to as ECM 1 to ECM 4. These categories describe the responsibilities and scope of maintenance work for different parts of the rail system:

- **ECM 1**: Responsible for the coordination and management of the entire maintenance process, including developing maintenance procedures and strategies, ensuring compliance with regulations and standards, and monitoring and controlling maintenance activities across the entire rail system.
- **ECM 2**: Focuses on the development of maintenance plans and strategies.
- **ECM 3**: Deals with the management of the rail vehicle fleet, including the planning and coordination of inspections, maintenance and repairs of rail vehicles to ensure that they meet requirements and can be operated safely.
- **ECM 4**: Covers the actual execution of maintenance work, including the maintenance and repair of rail vehicles in accordance with the maintenance plans and procedures developed.

### Digital Platforms and Software Solutions

Several companies offer software platforms to support ECM compliance:

1. **BOOM Rail Solutions**: They specifically target the roles of ECM 2, ECM 3, and ECM 4, offering corresponding functionalities in their software solution.

2. **ZEDAS (zedas®asset)**: zedas®asset is designed to comply with standards and regulations and to provide the best possible support for all ECM managers.(no content)The verification according to the Implementing Regulation (EU) 2019/779 is done automatically with zedas®asset ECM.

3. **Railnova (Railfleet)**: Railfleet ECM is Railnova's platform for ECM scheduling, delegation and traceability.(no content)Their Railfleet software provides a transparent planning and communication platform that helps fill the gap between the different ECM stakeholders.

4. **Siemens (Rail Service Hub)**: The Rail service hub was developed and validated with maintenance experts as an internal Siemens application, but is now offered to external users as Software-as-a-Service.(no content)The Rail service hub is part of Railigent X, Siemens Mobility's cloud-based application suite, which utilises IoT and AI to connect and optimise railway data, enhance system availability and reduce downtime.

5. **Alstom**: As a certified entity in charge of maintenance (ECM), Alstom takes railway systems under its responsibility and ensures safe and reliable operation by applying a consistent standard maintenance management system.(no content)Alstom uses the latest digital technologies including artificial intelligence, robotics, and digital twin to optimise maintenance activities.

### Regulatory Framework

The implementation of regulation (EU) 2019/779 has replaced and repealed regulation (EU) 445/2011.(no content)Since June 2022, certification has been mandatory for all rail vehicles in accordance with the ECM Regulation.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about train maintenance interval tracking methods:

## Train Maintenance Scheduling Approaches

### Three Main Methods

Rolling stock maintenance can be programmed in one of three ways: by mileage, by time, or by condition monitoring. Of these three methods, condition monitoring is the most recent. Traditionally, maintenance was carried out on a time basis, usually related to safety items like braking and wheel condition.

### Mileage-Based Maintenance

Many administrations later adopted a mileage-based maintenance system, although this is more difficult to operate as you have to keep records of all vehicle mileages, and this is time-consuming unless you have a modern train control and data gathering system.(no content)There is also the fact that a train will deteriorate just as quickly if it is stored unused somewhere as it would if it was being run in service every day. Only the items which deteriorate will vary.

### Time-Based/Calendar Maintenance

Time-based maintenance is scheduled at regular time intervals, such as every 6 months or once a year, regardless of how often the vehicle or equipment is used. It targets components that deteriorate over time, such as fluids, rubber hoses, seals, and belts, which can age and break down even when the vehicle is not in use.

### Condition-Based Monitoring

Condition-based monitoring is the most modern method used to determine train maintenance schedules, relying on automated data collection and sensors to trigger maintenance checks and processes based on the real-world performance of the vehicle.

### High-Speed Rail Example

The maintenance policy of China's HSR system belongs to the preventive maintenance type, which is defined as a set of activities aimed at improving the overall reliability and availability of a system. Under such a policy, high-speed trains need to conduct periodical inspection/overhaul after running for a certain time or mileage.

### Performance Metrics

Rolling stock performance in respect of failures can be measured by MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) or MDBF (Mean Distance Between Failures). It is sometimes measured by numbers of failures per year, month or week but this may not represent an accurate rate consistent with mileage.

### Modern Tracking Systems

Fleet management solutions automatically track the usage of every vehicle, so service managers can see exactly when each vehicle requires a service appointment. They can set up correctly-timed maintenance service intervals. This makes it easy to perform usage-based servicing without having to read and record odometers manually. Common metrics such as engine hours, fuel levels, and mileage can be used as benchmarks to help predict when a vehicle will need to be serviced next.

## Railway CMMS Defect Tracking and Fault Management

### Overview of CMMS in Railway Maintenance

Maintaining railway infrastructure is a multifaceted and vital responsibility that upholds the safety, reliability, and efficiency of rail services. The emergence of Computerized Maintenance Management Systems (CMMS) software has dramatically transformed this field, facilitating significant improvements in the management of tracks, signals, and rolling stock. This innovation has allowed the railway industry to optimize maintenance operations, improve predictive maintenance accuracy, and ensure timely and precise interventions.

### Defect Tracking and Fault Management

RailSmart RCM integrates real-time train monitoring, predictive maintenance, and defect management into a unified platform, providing actionable insights to ensure smooth rail operations.(no content)The system helps minimize asset downtime by keeping trains running smoothly with early detection of faults and timely interventions. It includes a fully integrated defect management system, helping operators detect and resolve issues quickly and effectively.(no content)ZEDAS's zedas®asset allows users to always have an eye on the current condition and status, warranty claims and the complete history of vehicles. The calculation of deadlines and the digital fault, order and warranty management facilitate workshop organisation.

### Workflow and Work Order Management

When failures occur, CMMS systems help manage and optimize the response to ensure quick and efficient repairs, reducing downtime. CMMS systems can automatically generate work orders when anomalies are detected or when scheduled maintenance is due, improving workflow efficiency and ensuring timely interventions.(no content)By automating routine maintenance tasks and creating a more streamlined workflow, CMMS significantly boosts the efficiency and productivity of maintenance teams. Technicians gain mobile access to work orders, instructions, and equipment histories, allowing them to perform tasks more effectively and minimize paperwork.(no content)CMMS provides a centralized platform for creating work orders, assigning tasks, and tracking progress. This ensures that maintenance activities are performed timely and proactively, reducing the risk of equipment failures and minimizing downtime.

### Corrective Maintenance Protocols

In case of asset failure, the system can automatically generate a work order and allocate the necessary resources, ensuring fast response times.(no content)Data on the individual rail vehicles obtained during the operating process is available for maintenance planning. The condition and load data as well as recorded damage and fault reports enable reliable, load-dependent maintenance planning.

### Status Tracking and Condition Monitoring

Trimble® CMMS™ (Condition Monitoring Management System) software is a condition monitoring data management platform that provides a unified environment for wayside detector data. Data from all types of wayside detectors including systems provided by Trimble and systems from other suppliers can be seamlessly integrated within CMMS.(no content)MaintWiz enables live condition tracking, enabling swift interventions before minor issues derail operations.

### AR-Assisted Maintenance Workflows

With augmented reality (AR) applications, the maintenance technician in the workshop is guided step by step through the maintenance process. Clearly defined workflows specify the exact sequence and positions at which the work steps are to be carried out.

### Regulatory Compliance

The railway industry operates under stringent regulatory frameworks that mandate strict adherence to safety and maintenance standards. To comply with these regulations, many railway companies utilize CMMS solutions. These systems enable precise record-keeping, thorough documentation of maintenance activities, and effective tracking of safety inspections and audits. By doing so, CMMS helps ensure that all regulatory requirements are met and industry standards are maintained.

# Railfleet Product Features Analysis

## Core Dashboard Components

**Operational Dashboard**: Displays "real-time access to important operational data such as asset status, restrictions, rolling stock location, key telematic values, events and fleet availability notifications."

**Maintenance Planning Dashboard**: Provides fleet managers and ECMs with oversight of maintenance events across rolling stock, allowing filtering by asset and bundling corrective/preventive tasks.

**Intervention Dashboard**: Consolidates fleet interventions with filtering capabilities by state (planned, late, in progress, done, cancelled), location, and timeframe.

## Key UI Elements & Features

- **Map Interface**: Enables asset location tracking, status filtering, geofencing alerts, and customizable points of interest (workshops, fuel stations)
- **Fleet Availability Module**: Uses Gantt chart visualization to display maintenance intervals and resource availability over time
- **Digital Train Logbook**: Tablet-accessible interface showing operational status, restrictions, and recent event history per train

## Scheduling & Status Visualization

- Real-time event notifications dispatched via email to stakeholders
- Downtime analysis tool with automatic defect classification (96% accuracy) using machine learning
- Counter reading module for manual preventive maintenance updates
- Campaign tracking dashboard for monitoring modification tasks across multiple assets

## Collaborative Features

The platform enables multi-stakeholder information sharing with role-based asset visibility and delegated permissions for ECM functions and maintenance event management.

# Boom Rail Workshop Software Features

## Core Functionality

**Workshop Management Process**
Boom Rail Workshop provides structured maintenance workflows spanning planning, scheduling, execution, and completion phases. The system enables "paperless work" through digital order management and mobile-based checklists accessed via smartphones.

## Key UI & Interface Components

**Workshop Monitor**
An intuitive dashboard allows workshop managers to coordinate orders electronically and assign them to working platforms, optimizing resource utilization.

**Mobile Solution**
Technicians receive work orders, maintenance specifications, and checklists digitally on mobile devices, eliminating paper-based documentation and reducing unproductive time.

**Integrated Resource Planning Interface**
Managers coordinate personnel, materials, tooling, and working platforms for each maintenance order within the system.

## Notable Features

- **ECM4 Compliance**: Seamless documentation of maintenance activities meeting Entity in Charge of Maintenance standards
- **Checklist-Based Processing**: Pre-defined mobile checklists with real-time data capture from measuring devices
- **Bidirectional Integration**: Interfaces with customer systems and Rail Asset for coordinated planning
- **Window-Based Scheduling**: Maintenance planning focused on vehicle stabling windows rather than individual orders
- **Automated Workflows**: Progresses through planning, preparation, execution, finalization, and completion stages

## Documentation & Accountability

The system captures photo documentation, comments, work logs, material consumption, and time records—all automatically integrated for invoicing and customer reporting.

## Core Interface Elements

Trapeze EAM for Rail includes several dashboard and visualization components:

**Visual Indicators & Icons:**
- Asset category icons representing "Rolling Stock," "Track," "Signals," "Power," and "Structures"
- Map-based visualization showing "vehicle location" for yard management
- Linear asset mapping with "interactive mapping" capabilities for MOW assets

## Work Order Management

The system features multi-trade work order functionality with:
- Real-time location tracking for track work (starting/stopping points)
- "Illustrated Parts Catalog for graphical parts selection"
- Mobile-enabled work orders functioning "online or offline"
- Condition-based maintenance triggering
- Automated work order routing to appropriate mechanics or crews

## Mobile Interface

**EAM Mobile** provides:
- Pre-trip inspection checklists with automated defect detection
- Offline capability with synchronization
- Service request and vehicle flagging triggers

## Dashboard & Reporting

The platform includes:
- KPI analysis dashboards specific to rail operations
- "FRA inspections performed by a qualified person with locomotive blue-card sign-off"
- Compliance reporting for FTA/FRA requirements
- Speed restriction and slow zone visualization

## Asset Status Management

Real-time displays include vehicle status, maintenance holds, and location tracking integrated with yard operations for on-time departure coordination.

# IVU Fleet Management: UI and Control Center Features

## Core Interface Components

**IVU.fleet <neo>** is the web-based control center offering a modern, unified dashboard. The system features a "one-screen interface" that consolidates essential operational data, enabling swift event management and providing quick situational awareness.

## Visualization and Display Elements

The platform employs "schematic and map-based display of the operations situation" for monitoring. This dual-view approach allows dispatchers to track vehicle locations and status through geographic visualization alongside schematic representations.

## Key Control Functions

The system maintains "continuous" communication between the background IVU.fleet system and driver-operated IVU.cockpit on-board software. Dispatchers can execute route modifications and journey scheduling adjustments "with just a few clicks," with automatic transmission to vehicles.

## Mobile Accessibility

As a web-based solution, IVU.fleet <neo> supports mobile device usage across control centers, field operations, and remote work environments, providing operational flexibility.

## Real-Time Monitoring

For electric bus operations specifically, the platform displays recharge status information and "continuously updated range forecasts," enabling proactive dispatch decisions before power depletion occurs.
